steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The equation $0.2t + 3 = 6$ models hair length in inches over time in months. The question asks what $0.2$ represents in this context. Since $0.2$ multiplies $t$ (months), its units are inches per month — it is the monthly growth rate.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — the number of months it takes to grow 0.2 inches: that would be a time value, not a coefficient. ✗
  • Choice B — the total length Antwon's hair grew: total growth depends on $t$; $0.2$ alone gives no total. ✗
  • Choice C — the length, in inches, Antwon's hair grows each month: $0.2$ inches/month times $t$ months yields inches. ✓
  • Choice D — the length, in inches, of Antwon's hair when he began growing it out: the starting length is 3 (the constant term), not 0.2. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
